body:
## Summary

When `box-dind` is run with host-image passthrough **enabled** (the default,
`DIND_HOST_PASSTHROUGH=public`) **and an explicit image allowlist set**
(`DIND_HOST_PASSTHROUGH_IMAGES=...`), but the host Docker socket is **not**
bind-mounted, passthrough is a **silent no-op**: the entrypoint copies nothing,
prints nothing, and the nested daemon stays empty. The operator clearly opted in
(they named the images they want passed through), yet the first nested
`docker run` re-pulls the full image from the registry with no hint as to why.

This is a deliberate design choice today — see the comment at
[`dind-entrypoint.sh` L401-L408](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/blob/b81aee7ab1dc2bda53733e80739e3b2284f38571/ubuntu/24.04/dind/dind-entrypoint.sh#L401-L408):

```sh
  if ! host_docker_available; then
    # A socket file exists but is unreachable: surface it. Otherwise the common
    # "no host socket mounted" case stays silent so the default mode is free.
    if [ -n "$DIND_HOST_DOCKER_SOCK" ] && [ -e "$DIND_HOST_DOCKER_SOCK" ]; then
      warn "host docker socket at ${DIND_HOST_DOCKER_SOCK} is not accessible; skipping passthrough"
    fi
    return 0
  fi
```

Staying silent for plain `box-dind` containers that never intended passthrough is
reasonable. But when the operator has set `DIND_HOST_PASSTHROUGH_IMAGES`, that is
an unambiguous opt-in signal that they **expect** passthrough to work — and a
missing socket means it silently won't. That mismatch is a footgun.

## Impact (real-world)

This bit us in `link-assistant/hive-mind`
([#1914](https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/issues/1914)): a Telegram
bot runs inside `konard/hive-mind-dind` (based on `konard/box-dind`) and launches
isolated tasks as nested `docker run konard/hive-mind-dind:latest`. The image is
**30+ GB**. The deploy intended host passthrough but the bot container's
`docker run` never mounted the host socket. Because passthrough was silent, the
empty nested daemon re-pulled the entire 30 GB image on the first isolated task —
with no log line anywhere explaining that passthrough had been skipped. It took a
source dive to find the missing `-v` flag.

## Reproducible example

```bash
# Host already has the image we want to reuse inside the nested daemon:
docker pull hello-world

# Run box-dind with passthrough ENABLED + an explicit allowlist, but WITHOUT
# mounting the host socket (the bug):
docker run --rm -it --privileged \
  -e DIND_HOST_PASSTHROUGH=public \
  -e DIND_HOST_PASSTHROUGH_IMAGES="hello-world" \
  konard/box-dind:latest \
  bash -lc 'docker run --rm hello-world'
# Observed: the nested daemon PULLS hello-world from the registry.
#           No warning that passthrough was skipped because no socket was mounted.

# Correct invocation (what the operator forgot) — works as intended:
docker run --rm -it --privileged \
  -v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/host-docker.sock:ro \
  -e DIND_HOST_PASSTHROUGH=public \
  -e DIND_HOST_PASSTHROUGH_IMAGES="hello-world" \
  konard/box-dind:latest \
  bash -lc 'docker run --rm hello-world'
# Observed: "passthrough loading host image: hello-world:latest" then reuse (no pull).
```

## Workaround

Mount the host socket read-only (this is the documented-but-easy-to-forget step):

```
-v /var/run/docker.sock:/var/run/host-docker.sock:ro
```

…or preload the image into the running container manually with
`docker save … | docker load`.

## Suggested fix (warn on opt-in + missing socket)

Keep the default-mode silence, but add a single warning when there is a clear
opt-in signal yet no socket. Minimal change to `passthrough_host_images`
([L401-L408](https://github.com/link-foundation/box/blob/b81aee7ab1dc2bda53733e80739e3b2284f38571/ubuntu/24.04/dind/dind-entrypoint.sh#L401-L408)):

```sh
  if ! host_docker_available; then
    if [ -n "$DIND_HOST_DOCKER_SOCK" ] && [ -e "$DIND_HOST_DOCKER_SOCK" ]; then
      # Socket file exists but is unreachable.
      warn "host docker socket at ${DIND_HOST_DOCKER_SOCK} is not accessible; skipping passthrough"
    elif [ -n "$DIND_HOST_PASSTHROUGH_IMAGES" ]; then
      # Operator opted in via an allowlist but no host socket is mounted: the
      # nested daemon will NOT be seeded and the first nested 'docker run' will
      # re-pull from the registry. Surface it instead of failing silently.
      warn "host-image passthrough is enabled and DIND_HOST_PASSTHROUGH_IMAGES is set, but no host docker socket is mounted at ${DIND_HOST_DOCKER_SOCK}; the nested daemon will NOT be seeded from the host (first 'docker run' will pull from the registry). Mount it with: -v /var/run/docker.sock:${DIND_HOST_DOCKER_SOCK}:ro"
    fi
    # Otherwise (no opt-in signal) the common "no host socket mounted" case stays
    # silent so plain box-dind containers are not spammed.
    return 0
  fi
```

Rationale for gating on `DIND_HOST_PASSTHROUGH_IMAGES` (rather than warning
whenever the default `public` mode has no socket): a non-empty allowlist is an
explicit "I want these images passed through" signal, so a missing socket is
almost certainly a misconfiguration worth surfacing — while plain `box-dind`
users who never touched passthrough see no new noise. (If you'd prefer, the same
warning could also fire when `DIND_HOST_PASSTHROUGH` was *explicitly* set by the
operator vs. defaulted, but the allowlist gate alone already covers the common
opt-in case and needs no "explicit vs default" tracking.)

Optionally, a one-line summary after a successful passthrough pass
(`passthrough: copied N, skipped M`) would further improve observability, but the
missing-socket warning above is the important fix.

## Notes

- box already handles the *present-but-unreachable* socket correctly (it warns).
  This request is only about the *absent* socket + explicit opt-in case.
- Downstream, we added a startup preflight in hive-mind that probes the nested
  daemon and prints this exact remediation, so we are covered operationally; this
  upstream change would help everyone who uses `box-dind` passthrough, not just us.
- Observed on `konard/box-dind` at v2.3.1 / main `b81aee7`.
title: dind passthrough: silent no-op when DIND_HOST_PASSTHROUGH_IMAGES is set but no host socket is mounted
